task.title: ELI5 code-commenting workflow for non-developer explainability

context.problem_statement: The existing learning-comments prompt helps learning developers, but AGW-078 targets non-developers who need to understand codebase intent, domain terms, and data flow without being taught programming syntax or receiving noisy inline comments.
background: workflows/prompts/codebase-learning-comments.md already maps docs, ADRs, implementation paths, and existing comments before proposing behavior-neutral comments.
AGW-044 remains separate and should cover a formal comment-only receipt proving no behavior changed.
README.md, docs/using-the-prompt-kit.md, and workflows/prompts/README.md already route users to the learning-comments prompt.
The new workflow should sharpen that prompt for non-developer explainability, not create a separate product surface or backlog app.
non_goals: Do not implement a static analysis engine or stale-comment scanner.
Do not implement the full AGW-044 receipt/schema work in this packet.
Do not add broad generated comments across a repository.
Do not teach general programming syntax, framework basics, or language tutorials.
Do not change runtime behavior, tests, dependencies, or source formatting except generated prompt adapters.
Do not modify repo-contract-kit in this source-side packet.

protected_files: repo-contract-kit checkout and installed kit templates
schemas/session-receipt.schema.json unless the implementer explicitly splits or absorbs AGW-044
scripts/ unless validation shows a prompt adapter/export bug
expected_outputs: Updated learning-comments prompt with a named non-developer or ELI5 mode.
Clear rules for when to add inline comments versus when to produce a separate explanation note.
A short explanation-note output section for non-developers.
Comment candidate rules that require sparse, evidence-backed, plain-language comments.
Explicit rejection rules for jargon, syntax tutoring, patronizing simplification, and comments that would mislead maintainers.
Updated prompt index/docs routing where needed.
Generated Codex prompt adapter in sync.
Backlog or summary updated only after implementation is complete.

acceptance_criteria.description: The prompt distinguishes learning developer support from non-developer explainability without assuming the reader will edit code.
The workflow requires agents to choose between inline comments and a separate explanation note, and to prefer the note when inline comments would make maintained source noisy.

The workflow defines high-value non-developer explanation targets: domain terms, business or research rules, data flow, external boundaries, state transitions, operational constraints, and surprising guardrails.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review the evidence and comment rules.

acceptance_criteria.description: The workflow rejects low-value ELI5 output: syntax narration, framework tutorials, obvious comments, unverifiable simplifications, anthropomorphic explanations, and jargon swaps that lose accuracy.
The final diff remains prompt/docs/backlog only, plus generated adapter output.
The generated Codex adapter matches the source prompt.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run make prompt-adapters-check or make validate.

acceptance_criteria.description: Documentation impact is covered in user-facing prompt docs or explicitly waived with a concrete reason.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run make docs-check and python3 scripts/check_doc_impact.py.

commands.expected_result: pass; existing warnings are acceptable if unchanged
pass after a patch bump, or with VERSION_CHECK_ALLOW_UNCHANGED=1 only if the final diff is clearly backlog-only

evidence_to_capture: Explanation of whether AGW-044 stayed separate or was intentionally absorbed.

docs_impact.notes: This changes a user-facing prompt workflow and how operators should run learning-comments mode.

known_risks: Inline comments for non-developers can pollute maintained source if the workflow does not strongly prefer sparse edits.
ELI5 language can become inaccurate if it over-simplifies domain, security, privacy, or architecture constraints.
This item can accidentally absorb AGW-044; keep formal receipt/schema enforcement separate unless explicitly approved.
Prompt changes can drift from the generated Codex adapter if export is skipped.
stop_conditions: The implementer needs to change schemas or receipt validation to prove comment-only behavior.
The implementer needs to modify repo-contract-kit templates or installer behavior.
The prompt cannot define a clear distinction between inline source comments and a separate explanation note.
Validation reveals generated adapter drift or docs-impact failure.

approval.notes: Yannick asked to flesh out AGW-078; implementation still needs a separate go or accepted task execution step.

handoff.next_packet_hint: If this lands cleanly, the follow-up packet should implement AGW-044 as a formal comment-only receipt or verifier so learning-comments mode can prove behavior neutrality.
